proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (a continuous variable in the range ~30–90) are associated with longer pfs_months in this CRC cohort, with a positive linear slope.
Patients with feature_051 == 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 == 0.
Patients with feature_038 == 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038 == 0.
pfs_months decreases monotonically as the ordinal feature_057 (levels 0, 1, 2) increases.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson/OLS slope: pfs_months increases by 0.177 per unit feature_078; very large effect (R^2 in univariate model implies r=+0.77).
Welch t-test: mean pfs_months 3.703 vs 5.055; difference -1.352 months (n1=27481, n0=22519).
Welch t-test: feature_038==1 patients have +0.972-month longer pfs_months (n1=10022, n0=39978).
Linear regression with feature_057 as numeric ordinal: slope -1.190 months per level; group means 5.27 (n=17592) -> 4.05 (n=24971) -> 2.91 (n=7437).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013 == 1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_013 == 0.
Patients with feature_043 == 1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_043 == 0.
Patients with the rare flag feature_109 == 1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_109 == 0.
Patients with the rare flag feature_067 == 1 have longer pfs_months than feature_067 == 0.
Higher values of the continuous variable feature_099 (range 0–~25) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher values of the continuous variable feature_092 (range ~1.5–5.5) are associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: difference -0.327 months (n1=20898, n0=29102).
Welch t-test: difference -0.305 months (n1=17657, n0=32343).
Welch t-test on a 4.5%-prevalence flag: difference -0.228 months (n1=2272, n0=47728).
Welch t-test on a 3%-prevalence flag: difference +0.221 months (n1=1506, n0=48494); rare positive predictor.
OLS slope on feature_099: -0.0730 months per unit; small but very precisely estimated negative effect.
OLS slope on feature_092: +0.465 months per unit; clear positive effect over a narrow numeric range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_057 (levels 0, 1, 2) and pfs_months departs from a strict linear trend; group means are not equally spaced.

analyses.result_summary: Nested F test of categorical vs linear feature_057. Linear-model means decline 5.27 -> 4.05 -> 2.91 (steps of -1.22 and -1.14, very close). F = 3.18, p = 0.075 — no significant departure from linearity at alpha=0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months has a non-zero quadratic component (i.e., the slope curves rather than being strictly linear).

analyses.result_summary: Adding I(feature_078**2) to the OLS model yields a quadratic coefficient of +0.00059 (p=6.99e-34); statistically significant but tiny — over a 60-unit span this curvature changes the slope by ~0.07 month/unit, so practical effect is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, and feature_043 are entered jointly, every coefficient retains its univariate sign and remains highly significant (i.e., these effects are largely additive rather than confounded by each other).

analyses.result_summary: 8-predictor OLS model. R^2 = 0.856. Coefficients (sign, p): feature_078 +0.176 (p≈0); feature_051 -1.36 (p≈0); feature_038 +0.94 (p≈0); feature_057 -1.17 (p≈0); feature_099 -0.076 (p≈0); feature_092 +0.47 (p≈0); feature_013 -0.33 (p≈0); feature_043 -0.29 (p≈0). All eight effects are independently significant with the expected signs.
analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_057 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043').fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 differs between feature_051 strata: specifically, the positive feature_078 slope is attenuated in patients with feature_051 == 1 (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_078:feature_051 = -0.0071 (p=1.90e-09); negative as predicted, meaning the per-unit feature_078 slope is ~-0.007 smaller when feature_051==1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_078 slope shrinks as feature_057 increases (negative feature_078 × feature_057 interaction); equivalently, feature_057's harmful effect is muted at high feature_078 values.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078:feature_057 = -0.0072 (p=1.30e-18); negative direction supports the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038 == 1 (longer pfs_months) is modified by feature_051 status; specifically, the feature_038 effect differs between feature_051 strata.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_038:feature_051 = +0.070 (p=0.151). Subgroup means (pfs_months): f038=0,f051=0: 4.87 (n=18049); f038=1,f051=0: 5.81 (n=4470); f038=0,f051=1: 3.50 (n=21929); f038=1,f051=1: 4.51 (n=5552). The two main effects appear largely additive — interaction not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038 == 1 is similar across feature_057 levels (no significant feature_038 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_038:feature_057 = +0.005 (p=0.89). Subgroup means: f057=0 -> 5.08/6.01 (off/on f038), f057=1 -> 3.86/4.85, f057=2 -> 2.73/3.63. The +1-month feature_038 benefit is consistent across strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_051 effect on pfs_months differs across feature_057 levels — specifically, the gap between feature_051==1 and feature_051==0 narrows as feature_057 increases (positive interaction in regression scale).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_051:feature_057 = +0.063 (p=0.019); modestly significant positive interaction, indicating slight attenuation of feature_051's negative effect at higher feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both feature_013==1 AND feature_043==1 have pfs_months that exceeds the additive prediction from each effect alone (positive interaction term, i.e., the two negative main effects sub-add).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_013:feature_043 = +0.452 (p=1.67e-25); strongly positive — the two negative main effects do not fully stack on top of each other.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_064 (race/ethnicity-coded) groups in this cohort, both unadjusted and after adjusting for the strongest predictors (feature_078, feature_051, feature_057).

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ANOVA across feature_064 levels (white=4.32 n=32456, black=4.27 n=5996, hispanic=4.31 n=7572, asian=4.31 n=3003, other=4.41 n=973); F-test p=0.479. After adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, joint F-test p=0.673. No detectable race effect on pfs_months in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_018 (insurance type) categories — patients on private insurance have longer pfs_months than uninsured/medicaid patients, both unadjusted and after adjusting for the strongest predictors.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted means: medicare=4.31 (n=21105), private=4.33 (n=19898), uninsured=4.25 (n=2006), medicaid=4.28 (n=6991); ANOVA p=0.22. Adjusted joint F-test p=0.22. Insurance type does not significantly predict pfs_months in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038==1 (longer pfs_months) is amplified at higher values of feature_078 (positive feature_078 × feature_038 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078:feature_038 = -0.0017 (p=0.280); the modest negative point estimate is non-significant. The feature_038 benefit appears roughly constant across feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_099 and pfs_months has a non-zero quadratic component (curving rather than strictly linear).
The negative feature_099 slope is steeper among feature_051==1 patients (negative feature_099 × feature_051 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Quadratic term I(feature_099**2) = -0.00031 (p=0.594); not significant. Linear approximation suffices for feature_099.
Interaction feature_099:feature_051 = +0.0061 (p=0.228); not significant — feature_099's negative effect is similar across feature_051 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_092 slope is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (negative feature_092 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_092:feature_057 = -0.0048 (p=0.86); not significant. feature_092 benefit is similar across feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Residual variance of pfs_months (after main-effect adjustment) differs across feature_057 levels — specifically the worst-prognosis stratum (feature_057==2) shows different variance than feature_057==0.

analyses.result_summary: Levene test on residuals from a model with feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_038, grouped by feature_057: residual SDs 0=0.984, 1=0.959, 2=0.977; W=3.66, p=0.026. Mild but significant variance heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive 3-way interaction feature_038 × feature_051 × feature_057 — the joint pattern of these three predictors yields larger pfs_months than additive expectation in some cells.

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction term = +0.088 (p=0.18); not significant. The 3-way structure does not exceed what 2-way and main effects already capture.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model containing main effects of feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_067 plus the interactions feature_038×feature_051, feature_038×feature_057, feature_078×feature_051 explains substantially more variance than the simpler 8-predictor main-effects model.

analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2 = 0.8574 (vs simpler model R^2 = 0.8561 in iteration 5); incremental R^2 ≈ 0.0013. Significant interactions retained: feature_078:feature_051 (-0.0067, p=1.24e-17), feature_038:feature_057 (-0.050, p=0.0004). feature_038:feature_051 was non-significant at p=0.068.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_109 on pfs_months persists after adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, and feature_057 (i.e., it is not explained away by these strong covariates).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_109 coefficient = -0.199 (p=4.54e-19); effect is ~13% smaller than unadjusted but remains highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_067 on pfs_months persists after adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, and feature_057.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_067 coefficient = +0.178 (p=6.03e-11); positive direction confirmed and significant in the adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of feature_038==1 vs ==0 is significant in BOTH feature_051==0 and feature_051==1 strata, with similar magnitude (~+0.9 to +1.0 month).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified Welch t-tests: feature_051==0 -> diff +0.941 (n1=4470, n0=18049, p=6.25e-95); feature_051==1 -> diff +1.011 (n1=5552, n0=21929, p=4.42e-140). Effect is consistently positive and similar in size in both strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_038 benefit is preserved across all three feature_057 levels (0, 1, 2), with similar magnitude (~+0.9 month) — i.e., the effect is not concentrated in any one stratum.
feature_089 (univariate p≈0.017) is not an independent predictor of pfs_months once feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, and feature_057 are adjusted for.
feature_102, feature_112, and feature_079 (all weak univariate signals) are similarly not independent predictors after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_089 = +0.0350 (p=0.029); only borderline independent signal.
Adjusted coefficients: feature_102 = -0.0040 (p=0.67); feature_112 = -0.0126 (p=0.23); feature_079 = -0.0005 (p=0.96). All three lose significance after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months across coarse bins of feature_078 (30–50, 50–60, 60–70, 70–80, 80–90) increases monotonically with roughly equal step size, consistent with a near-linear relationship.

analyses.result_summary: Bin means: 30-50 -> 1.08 (n=3355); 50-60 -> 2.65 (n=12225); 60-70 -> 4.29 (n=18920); 70-80 -> 5.95 (n=12207); 80-90 -> 7.78 (n=3293). Steps between successive bins: +1.57, +1.64, +1.66, +1.83 — strikingly linear (slope ≈ 0.17/unit, matching the global OLS slope from iteration 1).
